jokes.text: I've paid to air it during Super Bowl. -Wow. -Not on the same channel, of course.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth proudly announces airing during Super Bowl, impressive marketing
jokes.punchline: Deflating clarification that it's not on the actual Super Bowl channel
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-subversion with perfect timing. Economy is excellent - maximum deflation in minimum words.

jokes.text: I'm not Evans!
jokes.setup: Boss looking for Evans to deliver package
jokes.punchline: Worker desperately denying being Evans while clearly incompetent
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace incompetence gag, nothing special but functional

jokes.text: Our crew's replaceable. Your package isn't.
jokes.setup: Commercial promoting delivery service reliability
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest about treating employees as disposable
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant corporate honesty. Perfect economy and subverts typical advertising language beautifully.

jokes.text: -Are there really giant birds? -No, that was all special effects.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about the birds in the commercial
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth denies it right before being attacked by a giant bird
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony timing with visual payoff

jokes.text: [BIRD CRIES] [FARNSWORTH SCREAMS]
jokes.setup: Farnsworth just denied existence of giant birds
jokes.punchline: Immediate giant bird attack
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate contradiction visual gag

jokes.text: Caffeinated bacon. Baconated grapefruit. Admiral Crunch?
jokes.setup: Fry adjusting to 31st century breakfast options
jokes.punchline: Absurd food combinations and cereal name escalation
jokes.explanation: Good future food absurdity, nice escalation pattern

jokes.text: If you don't like that, try Archduke Chocula.
jokes.setup: Following up on cereal names
jokes.punchline: Count Chocula elevated to Archduke in the future
jokes.explanation: Clever elevation of familiar cereal character name

jokes.text: Ooh-ooh-ooh-ooh! [BENDER BURPS] -You!
jokes.setup: Amy eagerly volunteering to be captain
jokes.punchline: Farnsworth picks Fry after Bender's crude burp
jokes.explanation: Standard random selection gag

jokes.text: 'Death by airlock failure.' 'Death by brain parasite.' 'By sonic diarrhea.' -You don't want that.
jokes.setup: Reading through legal waivers for space delivery
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and disgusting death possibilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with sonic diarrhea being memorably gross

jokes.text: I don't know about previous captains... but I intend to do as little dying as possible.
jokes.setup: After reading list of horrible death possibilities
jokes.punchline: Fry's modest goal of minimal dying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry-esque understatement and practical approach to danger

jokes.text: I should warn you, though. He's a little unusual. He wears sandals.
jokes.setup: Warning about unusual doctor
jokes.punchline: Sandals being the concerning unusual trait
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - audience expects alien weirdness, gets footwear complaint

jokes.text: Open your mouth and let's look at that brain. No, no, not that mouth.
jokes.setup: Doctor wanting to examine brain through mouth
jokes.punchline: Implying Zoidberg has multiple mouths
jokes.explanation: Great alien anatomy humor that reveals Zoidberg's physiology

jokes.text: I only have one. Really? -Is there a human doctor around?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg surprised humans have one mouth
jokes.punchline: Leela's reasonable request for human doctor
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to alien weirdness

jokes.text: Young lady, I'm an expert on humans. Pick a mouth, open it and say-- [BUZZES]
jokes.setup: Zoidberg claiming expertise on humans
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates ignorance with multiple mouth reference
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Zoidberg's confident incompetence

jokes.text: [BUZZES] What?! My mother was a saint! Get out!
jokes.setup: Medical buzzer going off during examination
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg taking it as personal insult about his mother
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's emotional overreaction to medical equipment

jokes.text: Lord, Bender, you're filthy. Like you don't have crap in your neck.
jokes.setup: Amy commenting on Bender being dirty
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude comeback about Amy's neck
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender crudeness, in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Does it hurt when I go like this? Ow! A little.
jokes.setup: Amy cleaning Bender with pick, asking about pain
jokes.punchline: Bender's understated response to obvious pain
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with robotic understatement

jokes.text: I like having her around. She's the same blood type as me.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining why he likes Amy as intern
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about needing her blood
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economical. Perfect Farnsworth blend of casual and sinister.

jokes.text: What's your sorority? -Kappa Kappa Wong.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about her sorority
jokes.punchline: Sorority name playing on her last name Wong
jokes.explanation: Clever name-based humor that fits character

jokes.text: Hey, rich girl, look over here! It's me, Bender. I'm being entertaining. La-la-la, look at my head! It's all painted, look at my head! I got a big old head, and hey, ho! Show's over, I'm tired.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to get Amy's attention
jokes.punchline: Pathetic desperate performance that he immediately abandons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - attention-seeking followed by immediate laziness

jokes.text: Nice catch, idiot.
jokes.setup: Fry presumably drops something
jokes.punchline: Leela's sarcastic comment about his poor catch
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcastic insult, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: The moon? The moon moon? Wow! I'll be a famous hero. Like Neil Armstrong and other guys no one's heard of!
jokes.setup: Fry's excitement about going to the moon
jokes.punchline: Contradiction that Armstrong is famous but others aren't heard of
jokes.explanation: Good Fry-style confused logic about fame and space exploration

jokes.text: I'd like to hold off any major screwups... until at least my second day.
jokes.setup: Leela being careful about the mission
jokes.punchline: Low expectations for avoiding disasters
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's pragmatic pessimism

jokes.text: Nothing will go wrong. If something goes wrong, bring back the blood.
jokes.setup: Reassuring them nothing will go wrong
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting with blood request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth dark humor with excellent contradiction structure

jokes.text: Can I do the countdown? -Sure. Knock yourself out. -10, 9-- -Okay, we're here.
jokes.setup: Fry excitedly wanting to do launch countdown
jokes.punchline: Instant arrival cutting off his countdown
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of space travel expectations

jokes.setup: Fry determined to finish his countdown despite arriving
jokes.punchline: Pathetically completing it after the fact
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's determination despite being pointless

jokes.text: Hurry! I want to see the moon! -Relax, it's open till 9.
jokes.setup: Fry's excitement to see the moon
jokes.punchline: Leela treating the moon like a business with hours
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building humor about commercialized space

jokes.text: That's one small step for Fry-- And one giant line for admission.
jokes.setup: Fry attempting famous Armstrong quote
jokes.punchline: Leela's practical observation about the admission line
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on famous quote that deflates the moment perfectly

jokes.text: Can I have cuts? No.
jokes.setup: Fry asking to cut in line
jokes.punchline: Immediate flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple rejection gag with good economy

jokes.text: You won't believe it. There's an amusement park on the moon! It's the happiest place orbiting Earth.
jokes.setup: Fry's excitement about moon amusement park
jokes.punchline: Disney parody slogan adapted for space
jokes.explanation: Decent Disney parody with space twist

jokes.text: Let's dump it and say we delivered it. Too much work. Let's burn it and say we dumped it.
jokes.setup: Bender suggesting they fake the delivery
jokes.punchline: Even lazier solution of burning instead of dumping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender escalation of laziness, excellent character moment

jokes.text: If everyone's finished being stupid-- I had more, but you go ahead.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to restore order
jokes.punchline: Bender admitting he has more stupidity ready
jokes.explanation: Great Bender line showing self-awareness of his stupidity

jokes.text: Aye, aye, captain. I mean, Only One Eye. I mean, yes, sir. Ma'am.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to address Leela properly
jokes.punchline: Awkward stumbling over Leela's eye situation and gender
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Amy's social fumbling

jokes.text: My first space delivery. Greetings, Moon Man. We come in peace. I am Fry, from the planet Earth.
jokes.setup: Fry's first encounter with moon resident
jokes.punchline: Overly formal first contact speech to ordinary person
jokes.explanation: Good Fry characterization showing his sci-fi expectations vs reality

jokes.text: Wise guy. If I wasn't so lazy, I'd punch you in the stomach. -But you are lazy, right? -Oh, don't get me started.
jokes.setup: Moon resident threatening Fry but admitting laziness
jokes.punchline: Fry's logical question and resident's confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good lazy character gag with logical follow-up

jokes.text: I still have my self-respect. [LAUGHS] [CRIES]
jokes.setup: Mascot claiming self-respect
jokes.punchline: Immediate breakdown from laughter to crying
jokes.explanation: Good mascot misery gag with emotional whiplash

jokes.text: Who buys this trash? Idiots who need gifts for other idiots.
jokes.setup: Leela asking about souvenir quality
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of tourist market
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about tourist trap economics

jokes.text: I got you guys refrigerator magnets! Get it off! Get it off!
jokes.setup: Fry giving magnets as gifts
jokes.punchline: Bender's panicked reaction to magnet
jokes.explanation: Good setup for robot magnet weakness reveal

jokes.text: Keep those things off of me. Magnets screw up my inhibition unit.
jokes.setup: Bender's weird behavior from magnet
jokes.punchline: Technical explanation for his malfunction
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic explanation for personality malfunction

jokes.text: So you flip out and act like a crazy folk singer? Yes. I guess a robot would have to be crazy to want to be a folk singer.
jokes.setup: Fry clarifying Bender's malfunction
jokes.punchline: Bender's meta-commentary on folk music
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about folk music being inherently crazy

jokes.text: We're whalers on the moon We carry a harpoon There ain't no whales so we tell tall tales and sing a whaling tune
jokes.setup: Theme park whaling show on the moon
jokes.punchline: Admitting there are no whales but doing it anyway
jokes.explanation: Catchy absurd song that became iconic, perfect theme park logic

jokes.text: Bender! Bender! Over here! Oh, jeez. I went to high school with that guy.
jokes.setup: Someone calling Bender's name excitedly
jokes.punchline: Bender's embarrassed recognition
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassed recognition gag

jokes.text: Why does moon rock taste better than Earth rock? Because it's a little meatier.
jokes.setup: Comedy show riddle about moon rock
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'meteor' sounding like 'meatier'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but intentionally bad theme park comedy

jokes.text: This is weak. Address all complaints to the Monsanto Corporation.
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing the bad comedy
jokes.punchline: Corporate deflection of responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire with specific company reference

jokes.text: You're the kind of guy who visits Jerusalem... and doesn't see the Sexeteria.
jokes.setup: Bender criticizing Fry's priorities
jokes.punchline: Absurd Jerusalem sex attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line mixing high culture with base interests

jokes.text: Next year in Jerusalem!
jokes.setup: Following Bender's Jerusalem comment
jokes.punchline: Traditional Passover phrase as non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but somewhat random

jokes.text: Wow! I never realized the first astronauts were so fat. That's no astronaut. It's a TV comedian. He used space travel as a metaphor for beating his wife.
jokes.setup: Fry misunderstanding Jackie Gleason footage
jokes.punchline: Dark truth about Honeymooners domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Smart historical commentary on old TV show's dark subtext

jokes.text: -Leela's gonna kill me. -No, she'll probably make me do it.
jokes.setup: Fry worried about Leela's reaction
jokes.punchline: Bender's darker interpretation of delegation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's view of his relationship with Leela.

jokes.text: Could you get those keys out for me? What do I look like, a guy who's not lazy?
jokes.setup: Fry asking Bender to help retrieve keys
jokes.punchline: Bender's rhetorical question affirming his laziness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization with great rhetorical structure

jokes.text: We're whalers on the moon We carry a harpoon There ain't no whales so we tell tall tales and sing a whaling tune
jokes.setup: Same whaling song from before
jokes.punchline: Repetition as running gag
jokes.explanation: Effective running gag building familiarity with catchy tune

jokes.text: That's not how it happened. I don't see you with a fun-gineering degree.
jokes.setup: Fry correcting historical inaccuracy
jokes.punchline: Guide's dismissive fake engineering degree
jokes.explanation: Good theme park fake expertise wordplay

jokes.text: I died doing what I loved.
jokes.setup: Bender apparently in danger
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of death while being lazy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line with excellent economy and character truth

jokes.text: We're gonna die! It's every man for himself! Help me, Leela!
jokes.setup: Fry declaring every man for himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking Leela for help
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction showing Fry's panic logic

jokes.text: Come on, it's just like making love. You know, left, down, rotate 62 degrees, engage rotor.
jokes.setup: Bender comparing crane operation to sex
jokes.punchline: Mechanical technical instructions for lovemaking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bender line showing his robotic perspective on human intimacy

jokes.text: I know how to make love. Oh!
jokes.setup: Fry defending his knowledge
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradictory evidence
jokes.explanation: Good immediate contradiction gag

jokes.text: That's her, officers! That's the woman who programmed me for evil!
jokes.setup: Bender pointing out someone to authorities
jokes.punchline: Blaming someone else for his evil programming
jokes.explanation: Good Bender blame-shifting and robot humor

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I'm gonna go build my own theme park... with blackjack and hookers. In fact, forget the park.
jokes.setup: Bender threatening to build competing theme park
jokes.punchline: Adding inappropriate elements then abandoning the park entirely
jokes.explanation: Iconic Bender line that became internet meme. Perfect escalation and character truth.

jokes.text: Trespassers, eh? -No, we're amusement park patrons. That's a wicked, sinful place. Tilt-A-Whirl's okay, but the rest is mighty wicked.
jokes.setup: Farmer condemning amusement park as sinful
jokes.punchline: Making exception for Tilt-A-Whirl specifically
jokes.explanation: Great absurd moral distinction between rides

jokes.text: Borrow? Looky here, city girl. Oxygen don't grow on trees.
jokes.setup: Request to borrow oxygen
jokes.punchline: Farmer's ironic statement about oxygen not growing on trees
jokes.explanation: Clever irony since oxygen literally does come from trees

jokes.text: Night lasts two weeks on the moon. Yep. Drops down to minus 173. Fahrenheit or Celsius? First one, then the other.
jokes.setup: Explaining moon's extreme cold
jokes.punchline: Gets so cold it goes through both temperature scales
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke about extreme temperature

jokes.text: Robot daughters? -This here is Lulabelle Seven. -Yoo-hoo! -Daisy Mae 128K. -Yoo-hoo! And the Crush-A-Nator! Yoo-hoo. Whoo.
jokes.setup: Moon farmer introducing robot daughters
jokes.punchline: Names escalating from quaint to technical to threatening
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of robot names from folksy to intimidating

jokes.text: Face it. The moon's a dump. A boring, dried-up wasteland. The only reason people come is for the tacky amusement park. -Can't you accept that?
jokes.setup: Leela's blunt assessment of the moon
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest tourism critique
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Leela's pragmatism vs Fry's romanticism

jokes.text: I'll learn you to sleep with my robot daughters! -He'll never find me in here!
jokes.setup: Farmer angry about Bender with daughters
jokes.punchline: Bender hiding while making noise
jokes.explanation: Standard chase comedy setup

jokes.text: You didn't touch the Crush-A-Nator, did you? Of course not. A lady that fine, you gotta romance first.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if Bender touched the robot
jokes.punchline: Bender treating robot like a real romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good Bender characterization showing his robot relationships

jokes.text: Goldarn it, Crush-A-Nator! Jump! No, Pa. I love him.
jokes.setup: Farmer ordering robot daughter to jump
jokes.punchline: Robot declaring love for Bender
jokes.explanation: Good robot romance absurdity

jokes.text: Hey, cool! Dark side of the moon. Nightfall's coming. -Hurry, before we freeze! -What do you mean 'we,' mammal?
jokes.setup: Warning about freezing temperatures
jokes.punchline: Bender excluding himself as a non-mammal robot
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic about robot vs mammal survival needs

jokes.text: Oh, dear. I really ought to do something. But I am already in my pajamas.
jokes.setup: Farnsworth aware his crew is in danger
jokes.punchline: Pajamas as excuse for not helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Farnsworth selfishness with absurd prioritization

jokes.text: My foot's bigger. Isn't this the greatest thing you've ever seen?
jokes.setup: Finding Neil Armstrong's footprint
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual comparison and enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's irreverent attitude toward historical artifacts.

jokes.text: Oh, no room for Bender, huh? Fine. I'll build my own lunar lander, with blackjack and hookers. In fact, forget the lunar lander and the blackjack. Screw the whole thing.
jokes.setup: Bender excluded from shelter
jokes.punchline: Callback to theme park threat with same escalating abandonment
jokes.explanation: Great callback that reinforces Bender's character pattern

jokes.text: She'll be coming around the mountain when she comes I'll kill you, Amy!
jokes.setup: Bender singing folk song due to magnet
jokes.punchline: Leela's threat interrupting his performance
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Bender's magnet-induced folk singing
